ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: BLOCK

ac:rich-text-body.p: Frequently asked questions about the SKA Science Operations Portal, data access, proposals, tools, and community resources.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: How do I create an account on the Science Operations Portal?

ac:rich-text-body.p: To create an account, visit the Portal registration page and provide your name, email, institution, and research interests. You will receive a confirmation email with a secure activation link. Once activated, your account provides access to proposal submission tools, data discovery, and community resources.
Account creation is free and open to researchers at all levels (undergraduate, graduate, postdoctoral, faculty) from institutions worldwide.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Do I need an account to access SKA data?

ac:rich-text-body.p: No. Public SKA data (released to the community) can be discovered and downloaded without an account via the SKA Archive portal. However, creating an account allows you to:

ul.li: Save searches and create custom data discovery workflows
Manage proprietary data access during embargo periods
Submit proposals and track their status
Upload and share Advanced Data Products

ac:rich-text-body.p: An account is required to submit proposals.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: What are the first steps after registering?

ac:rich-text-body.p: After registering and activating your account, we recommend:

ol.li: Complete your user profile with your research interests and institutional affiliation
Review the Introduction to Science Verification to understand SKA's readiness status
Explore the Introduction to Observatory Data Products and Advanced Data Products to learn about available data types
Visit the Archive portal and perform a test search in your science area
Consult the getting started tutorials for proposal submission or data analysis

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: How do I submit a proposal?

ac:rich-text-body.p: Log in to the Science Operations Portal with your account and navigate to the Proposal Submission page. The portal provides an interactive proposal editor with:

ul.li: Guided templates for different science categories
Real-time validation of technical specifications
Tools for estimating observation time and sensitivity using the SKA Exposure Calculator
Ability to add co-investigators and manage author roles

ac:rich-text-body.p: Proposals must be submitted before the published deadline (typically ~3 months before observations). Submission information appears in the Portal's "Calls for Proposals" section.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: What is the proposal review process?

ac:rich-text-body.p: SKA proposals undergo rigorous scientific review:

ol.li: Initial Triage: Proposals are checked for completeness and technical feasibility
Panel Review: Assigned to scientific panels matching the proposal's science category
External Review: Independent reviewers provide detailed scientific assessment
Ranking: Proposals are ranked by panel based on scientific merit and technical feasibility
Time Allocation: Available observing time is allocated based on rankings, subject to scheduling constraints

ac:rich-text-body.p: The review process typically takes 3–4 months. You will receive notification of the outcome and feedback from reviewers.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: When are observation proposals due?

ac:rich-text-body.p: Proposal deadlines are announced approximately 6 months in advance and typically occur 2–3 times per year. The exact schedule and deadlines are posted on the Portal's "Calls for Proposals" page and in the Portal newsletter.
To stay informed, subscribe to Portal announcements or check the news section regularly.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Can I submit a joint proposal with colleagues at other institutions?

ac:rich-text-body.p: Yes. The proposal editor allows you to add co-investigators and collaborators from any institution. Designate one investigator as the Principal Investigator (PI) — this person is responsible for submitting the proposal and will be the primary contact for communication about the proposal's outcome.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Where do I find SKA data?

ac:rich-text-body.p: All publicly released SKA data are discoverable and accessible via the SKA Science Archive, integrated within the Science Operations Portal. Use the Archive's search interface to browse by:

ul.li: Sky coordinates (RA/Dec) or object names
Frequency band and bandwidth
Observation date or date range
Data product type (images, spectra, visibilities, timing data)
Observer name or project identifier
Quality metrics and processing flags

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: What is a proprietary period?

ac:rich-text-body.p: Data from observations have a proprietary period (typically 12 months) during which only the proposal team can access the data. This ensures the proposal team has time to conduct their primary science without competition.
After the proprietary period expires, data are released to the public and become discoverable in the Archive. You can view the proprietary period end date for any observation in the Archive metadata.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: How do I download SKA data?

ac:rich-text-body.p: From the Archive, select the dataset(s) you wish to retrieve and click "Download." The Archive offers several access modes:

ul.li: Direct download: Download full files to your local machine via HTTPS or rsync
Streaming: Access subsets of large files without downloading everything (e.g., specific frequency ranges or time windows)
Cloud compute: Analyse data in situ using SKA computational services without local download

ac:rich-text-body.p: For very large datasets, cloud-based analysis is often more efficient than downloading terabytes locally.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Can I access data during the proprietary period?

ac:rich-text-body.p: Only the proposal team (PI and registered co-investigators) can access data during the proprietary period. Access is authenticated via your Portal account.
If you are not on the proposal team and need access to proprietary data, contact the PI to request data access or collaborative opportunities.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: What is SRCnet?

ac:rich-text-body.p: SRCnet (SKA Regional Computing Network) is a distributed computing infrastructure providing researchers with cloud-based access to SKA data and computational resources for large-scale analysis.
Rather than downloading multi-terabyte datasets, you can:

ul.li: Run analysis jobs directly on archive-hosted data
Access pre-installed analysis software and libraries
Scale computations across multiple nodes for faster processing
Store intermediate results in the cloud without local infrastructure

ac:rich-text-body.p: SRCnet is free for academic researchers. Details and getting started guides are available in the Portal.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: What is the SKA Exposure Calculator?

ac:rich-text-body.p: The SKA Exposure Calculator (also called the Sensitivity Calculator) is an interactive tool that estimates:

ul.li: Observation time required to reach a target sensitivity
Expected sensitivity (noise level) for a given integration time
Array configuration effects on image quality and resolution

ac:rich-text-body.p: The calculator is essential for proposal preparation, helping you justify requested observing time and validate that your science goals are achievable. It is accessible from the Portal's Tools section.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: What is the Proposal Handling Tool (PHT)?

ac:rich-text-body.p: The Proposal Handling Tool (PHT) is the integrated proposal submission and management system within the Science Operations Portal. It provides:

ul.li: Step-by-step proposal editor with science-specific templates
Integration with the Exposure Calculator for scheduling and sensitivity estimates
Real-time validation of technical parameters
Submission tracking and outcome notifications
History and archive of past proposals

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Are there planning tools for observations?

ac:rich-text-body.p: Yes. The Portal provides observation planning tools for proposal teams to:

ul.li: Visualise target positions and observability windows
Check for Radio Frequency Interference (RFI) in observing bands
Calculate expected resolution and sensitivity for specific array configurations
Schedule observations accounting for maintenance windows and array reconfiguration

ac:rich-text-body.p: These tools are available to logged-in users and are updated regularly as the telescope commissioning progresses.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Who can use the SKA?

ac:rich-text-body.p: The SKA is an international observatory open to researchers from all countries and institutional types. There are no citizenship, visa, or institutional restrictions. PhD students, postdoctoral researchers, faculty, and independent researchers are all eligible to submit proposals.
The only requirement is to register for a Portal account and submit a proposal that passes scientific review.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: How should I cite SKA data or results in publications?

ac:rich-text-body.p: When publishing results based on SKA data, please:

ol.li: Cite the observatory: Use the standard SKA observatory citation provided in the Archive metadata for each dataset
Include archive reference: Cite the archive DOI or unique identifier for the data used
Acknowledge funding: Acknowledge support from the SKA Observatory and any regional computing facilities used (e.g., SRCnet)
Data availability statement: Include a statement describing how readers can access the data, e.g., "Data are available from the SKA Science Archive at [URL] under accession [ID]."

ac:rich-text-body.p: Templates for citations and acknowledgments are provided in the Portal's Publication Resources section.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Where can I find community resources and tutorials?

ac:rich-text-body.p: The Science Operations Portal hosts an extensive Resource Center including:

ul.li: Tutorials and getting started guides: Walks through proposal submission, data discovery, and analysis workflows
Jupyter notebooks: Example analyses for common science cases
Software and containers: Pre-built analysis environments (Docker, Singularity) with standard radio astronomy tools
Data reduction pipelines: Documented workflows for processing and analysing SKA data
Community wiki: Curated knowledge base maintained by the SKA user community
Helpdesk and FAQ: Contact support and search for answers to common questions

ac:rich-text-body.p: All resources are freely available and regularly updated.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: How can I get help if I have questions?

ac:rich-text-body.p: Help and support are available through multiple channels:

ul.li: Portal help desk: Submit support tickets via the Portal's Help section. Typical response time is 1–2 business days
Community forum: Ask questions and share knowledge with other SKA users (monitored by Portal staff)
Email support: For urgent issues, email the SKA Observatory support team (address in Portal footer)
Documentation: Comprehensive documentation and FAQs covering most common questions

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Is there a community newsletter or news feed?

ac:rich-text-body.p: Yes. The Science Operations Portal publishes a regular newsletter featuring:

ul.li: Announcement of proposal deadlines and observation calls
Updates on new data releases from Science Verification
New features and improvements to Portal tools
Science highlights and recent discoveries
Community events and training opportunities

ac:rich-text-body.p: Subscribe to the newsletter from your Portal account settings. You can also follow @SKA_Observatory on social media for real-time updates.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: More Questions?

ac:rich-text-body.p: If your question is not answered here, visit the Help section of the Science Operations Portal or contact the SKA Observatory support team. We are committed to supporting your science with the SKA.
